name: Scallops with Mushrooms in Wine
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, bring stock, wine, shallots, celery, cilantro, bay leaf, and peppercorns to a boil.
2. Simmer uncovered for 30 minutes.
3. Strain the liquid and return it to the saucepan. Add scallops and mushrooms. If using sea scallops instead of bay scallops, slice them in half.
4. Mushrooms may be sliced or cut in half. Simmer covered for 5 minutes.
5. Strain scallops and mushrooms well and place in a large bowl.
6. Quickly reduce the poaching liquid to about 1 cup in another saucepan over moderate heat.
7. Melt butter in the saucepan. When foaming subsides, reduce heat and stir in flour until no further foam remains. Do not brown.
8. Remove from heat and slowly pour in the reduced poaching liquid and milk while stirring vigorously. Return to heat and bring to a boil with continuous stirring.
9. Cook for 1 minute. Whisk cream and eggs together in a bowl.
10. Add 2 tablespoons of hot sauce to the egg mixture with stirring, then add 2 more.
11. Slowly pour the egg mixture into the sauce while stirring, bringing back to a boil for 30 seconds.
12. Remove from heat, add lemon juice, ground pepper, and 1 teaspoon chicken base. Stir in about half of the sauce over the scallops gently.
13. Butter six shallow, 4-inch baking dishes. Spoon scallops into each dish and cover with remaining sauce.
14. Heat in a preheated oven at 375°F for about 10 minutes.
15. Mix topping ingredients together and sprinkle over the scallops. Place under the broiler until the topping is slightly brown. If individual dishes are not available, use an 8-inch casserole dish.
